sectionName: Biological & Biochemical Foundations of Living Systems

questions.passage: Clinicians compared thyroid-axis measurements in three groups. Primary hypothyroid patients had high TSH, low free T4, and reduced basal metabolic rate. Hyperthyroid patients had suppressed TSH, high free T4, and increased basal metabolic rate. Imaging showed intact pituitary tissue in both patient groups.
questions.stem: Which pattern best identifies primary hypothyroidism?

choices.B: High TSH with low free T4
choices.A: Low TSH with high free T4
choices.C: Low TSH with low free T4 due to pituitary failure
choices.D: Normal TSH with absent thyroid hormone receptors

explanations.B: Correct. Primary hypothyroidism means thyroid hormone production is low, so pituitary TSH rises by negative feedback.
explanations.A: Low TSH with high T4 matches hyperthyroidism.
explanations.C: Low TSH with low T4 would suggest central hypothyroidism, not primary thyroid failure.
explanations.D: The figure directly shows abnormal TSH/T4 rather than isolated receptor absence.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 1. Thyroid-axis measurements in control and patient groups.

questions.stem: Why is TSH suppressed in hyperthyroidism?

choices.C: High circulating thyroid hormone negatively feeds back on pituitary TSH release
choices.A: The pituitary is absent in all hyperthyroid patients
choices.B: TSH is converted into T4 in peripheral tissues

explanations.C: Correct. Elevated thyroid hormone suppresses TRH/TSH release through negative feedback.
explanations.A: The passage states pituitary tissue is intact.
explanations.B: TSH stimulates thyroid hormone production; it is not converted into T4.
explanations.D: T4 is high in the hyperthyroid group.

questions.stem: The increased basal metabolic rate in hyperthyroidism is most consistent with thyroid hormone increasing:

choices.A: Transcription of genes involved in metabolic activity
choices.B: Urinary glucose loss as its only mechanism

explanations.A: Correct. Thyroid hormone acts through intracellular receptors to alter transcription and increase metabolic activity.
explanations.B: Urinary glucose loss is not the main thyroid hormone mechanism.
explanations.C: Mature red blood cells lack nuclei.
explanations.D: Bacterial conjugation is unrelated to thyroid physiology.

questions.stem: A pituitary tumor secreting excess TSH would most likely cause:

choices.D: High TSH with high free T4
choices.A: High TSH with low free T4 identical to primary hypothyroidism
choices.B: Low TSH with low free T4
choices.C: No change in thyroid hormone levels

explanations.D: Correct. Excess pituitary TSH would stimulate the thyroid, increasing T4 despite already high TSH.
explanations.A: Primary hypothyroidism has high TSH because T4 is low, not because TSH is autonomous.
explanations.B: Low TSH/low T4 suggests central deficiency.
explanations.C: TSH stimulates thyroid hormone production, so changes are expected.

questions.stem: Because thyroid hormone is lipid-soluble, its receptor is most likely located:

choices.C: Inside the cell, where it can regulate transcription
choices.B: As a ligand-gated sodium channel at the NMJ

explanations.C: Correct. Thyroid hormone receptors are intracellular/nuclear receptors that regulate gene transcription.
explanations.A: The intestinal lumen is not the receptor location.
explanations.B: Ligand-gated sodium channels describe receptors such as nicotinic ACh receptors, not thyroid hormone receptors.
explanations.D: Bacterial plasmids are unrelated to human thyroid hormone signaling.

questions.passage: Researchers treated cultured adipocytes with TNF-alpha, an inflammatory cytokine. TNF-alpha increased NF-kB activity and reduced insulin-stimulated glucose uptake and Akt phosphorylation. Adding an NF-kB pathway inhibitor partially rescued insulin signaling. Treating cells with IL-10, an anti-inflammatory cytokine, did not reduce glucose uptake.
questions.stem: What effect did TNF-alpha have on adipocyte insulin signaling?

choices.B: It decreased insulin-stimulated glucose uptake and Akt phosphorylation
choices.A: It increased Akt phosphorylation above control
choices.D: It converted adipocytes into pancreatic beta cells

explanations.B: Correct. The figure shows TNF-alpha lowers glucose uptake and Akt phosphorylation while increasing NF-kB activity.

questions.passageImageCaption: Figure 2. Inflammatory signaling and insulin responsiveness in adipocytes.

questions.stem: Akt phosphorylation is downstream of which hormone pathway in this passage?

explanations.D: Correct. Insulin receptor signaling activates PI3K/Akt pathways that promote glucose uptake.
explanations.A: Glucagon primarily signals through GPCR/cAMP pathways in liver.
explanations.B: Thyroid hormone regulates transcription through intracellular receptors, not this adipocyte Akt readout.
explanations.C: C5a is a complement chemotactic factor, not the insulin pathway measured.

questions.stem: The inhibitor partially rescuing insulin signaling suggests:

choices.A: NF-kB activation contributes to TNF-alpha-induced insulin resistance
choices.B: NF-kB is required for all glucose uptake under control conditions
choices.D: IL-10 and TNF-alpha have identical effects

explanations.A: Correct. Blocking NF-kB improves uptake and Akt phosphorylation under TNF-alpha, supporting a causal contribution to insulin resistance.
explanations.B: The data do not show complete dependence of basal uptake on NF-kB.
explanations.C: TNF-alpha reduces insulin signaling in the figure.
explanations.D: IL-10 does not reduce glucose uptake like TNF-alpha.

questions.stem: Which glucose transporter is most relevant to insulin-stimulated uptake in adipocytes?

explanations.B: Correct. Insulin promotes GLUT4 translocation in adipose tissue and skeletal muscle.
explanations.A: SGLT2 is a kidney sodium-glucose cotransporter.

questions.stem: Why is IL-10 included as a useful comparison?

choices.C: It helps distinguish pro-inflammatory TNF-alpha effects from cytokine exposure in general
choices.A: It proves all cytokines reduce insulin signaling
choices.B: It directly measures thyroid hormone secretion
choices.D: It eliminates the need for a control group

explanations.C: Correct. An anti-inflammatory cytokine that does not impair uptake helps show the TNF-alpha effect is not simply due to any cytokine treatment.
explanations.A: IL-10 does not reduce signaling in the figure.
explanations.B: IL-10 treatment in adipocytes does not measure thyroid secretion.
explanations.D: A control group remains necessary for baseline comparison.
